items.title: Italy bans Kanye West, Travis Scott concerts over security concerns
items.summary_auto: Italy has banned two concerts involving American rappers Kanye West and Travis Scott that were due to take place in July in the northern city of Reggio Emilia, authorities said on Saturday.

items.title: Rescuers free 4 men trapped in flooded cave in Laos, search for 2 still missing
items.summary_auto: Rescuers in Laos said Saturday they have safely evacuated four villagers trapped in a flooded cave for 10 days. One person was safely released a day earlier.

items.title: Trump remains in excellent health despite leg swelling, hand bruising, White House memo says
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump, who turns 80 in June and is the oldest person to assume the presidency, frequently casts himself as more energetic and fitter than Joe Biden, his Democratic predecessor.

items.title: 'He was a changed person': Clinton Ellison, survivor of N.S. mass shooting, dead at 52
items.summary_auto: Ellison's family says his mental health never recovered after witnessing the 2020 tragedy, which claimed the life of his brother.

items.title: Did Trump take tomato? Here's what's behind the latest grocery price sticker shock
Thousands are now Canadians under new citizenship law. Half of them are Americans
items.summary_auto: Thousands of people around the world have received Canadian citizenship certificates as a result of a change late last year to federal law. Half of them Americans.

items.title: Loop's reusable packaging revolution failed in Canada. Here's why it's thriving in 500 French grocery stores
items.summary_auto: Seven years ago, Tom Szaky got some of the world’s biggest corporations to invest in returnable containers, but the pilots shuttered everywhere but France. Could what he's learned there allow for a Canadian comeback?

items.title: Chinese tourists are choosing Canada — again
items.summary_auto: The "people-to-people" ties between Canada and China are seeing a surge as geopolitical relations between the two countries warm up. As China lifts restrictions on group tours to Canada and Canada is increasing flights to China, tourism between the two countries is up.

items.title: Donald Trump turns 80 soon. Questions about his health aren't going away
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump celebrates his 80th birthday in June, and will do so amid questions about whether his physical health and mental sharpness are on the decline. A memo from his physician that was released by the White House on Friday says Trump ‌remains in excellent health.

items.title: Trudeau's climate policy architects gather as Carney changes course
items.summary_auto: Former Prime Minister Justin Trudeau and his former climate change cabinet ministers — Catherine McKenna, Jonathan Wilkinson and Steven Guilbeault — on Wednesday were at the Rideau Club, a private social club near Parliament Hill.

items.title: All for one: Norway, Germany pitch Canada on shared submarine fleet
items.summary_auto: Germany and Norway are intensifying their bid for Canada’s submarine contract, pitching the Type 212CD as part of a shared NATO fleet. The allies have even surrendered production slots to accelerate delivery, highlighting a growing effort to counter South Korea's advantage.

items.title: Wildfires are destroying trees faster than we are replacing them
items.summary_auto: Canada is losing trees faster than nature can grow them or people can plant them. A northern Manitoba tree-planting program is trying to replace trees destroyed by wildfires, but the cancellation of the federal Two Billion Trees Program is making that more challenging.

items.title: A look back on the Habs' playoff run — and how it energized the city
items.summary_auto: The Montreal Canadiens' Stanley Cup dream is over — for now. But here's a look at how this playoff run took Habs fever to a level unseen in recent years.

items.title: Canadiens eliminated from playoffs after blowout loss to Hurricanes
items.summary_auto: The Hurricanes won the best-of-seven series 4-1 with four straight victories. They will be heading to the Stanley Cup final for the first time since 2006.

items.title: Ailing Davies, Shaffelburg part of 26-man team as Canada's World Cup roster revealed
items.summary_auto: Canada coach Jesse Marsch revealed his roster for the men's World Cup on Friday, with ailing 'Maritime Messi' Jacob Shaffelburg a surprise inclusion over Daniel Jebbison.

items.title: Canada continuing genocide against Indigenous Peoples, international tribunal finds
items.summary_auto: An international tribunal has ruled that the Government of Canada’s current policies constitute an ongoing genocide against Indigenous Peoples, following a grueling week of hearings on intergenerational trauma.

items.title: Fidel Castro's anti-communist daughter hopes the U.S. will bring regime change to Cuba
items.summary_auto: While thousands of Cubans have been marching in the streets to oppose the U.S. indictment of their former president Raúl Castro, a literal daughter of the revolution is singing a different tune.

items.title: Bishnoi gang sent letter to B.C. police force claiming 1,000 foot soldiers ready to carry out extortions
Canada Post records Q1 loss of $205 million amid major restructuring
items.summary_auto: The loss comes amid an ongoing labour dispute with workers that the Crown corporation attributed in part to the downturn in its parcel business.

items.title: Federal judge blocks closure of Kennedy Center, says Trump name must be dropped
items.summary_auto: A U.S. federal judge ruled Friday that U.S. President Donald Trump's name was illegally added to the Kennedy Center and blocked the administration from closing the cultural and arts venue for major renovations — the latest legal setback for Trump's efforts to leave his personal mark on the landscape of the nation's capital.

items.title: Carney, Pope Leo discuss responsible AI after pontiff warns world to slow development
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney spoke with Pope Leo about artificial intelligence and the "imperative that AI must serve humanity" — a conversation that comes days after the Pope urged governments to slow down the development of AI systems.

items.title: Stellantis is launching 11 new North American models. Could they be built in Canada?
items.summary_auto: Global automaker Stellantis' recently revealed plans to launch 11 new vehicle models in North America "produces opportunities" for those models to be built in Ontario, the head of the company's Canadian division says.

items.title: Mayor questions why police brought suspected WW II-era explosive to Stephenville, N.L.
items.summary_auto: Stephenville Mayor Bob Byrnes says it's "concerning" the RCMP brought the explosive to a residential neighbourhood, sparking distress in the community. Military experts agree: it was the wrong decision.

items.title: Supreme Court clarifies trial time limits to avoid having charges dropped over delays
items.summary_auto: The Supreme Court of Canada dismissed two challenges to the Jordan framework on Friday, ruling that the time limits put on criminal trials before charges be thrown out of court are justified under Section 11(b) of the Charter.

items.title: 'Exchange of gunfire' after man took 3 hostages in Winnipeg home, police say
items.summary_auto: Winnipeg police say a man took three teens hostage inside a St. James home on Friday, leading to an 'exchange of gunfire' as officials tried to arrest him.

items.title: ICE agent who faces charges in Minnesota shooting arrested in Texas
items.summary_auto: The top prosecutor from Minnesota's Hennepin County announced the arrest Friday of an ICE officer charged for shooting a Venezuela-born man in January at the height of a deadly immigration blitz launched by the Donald Trump administration.

items.title: Banff, Calgary urge caution along Bow River as water levels rise
items.summary_auto: It is set to be a wet weekend across southern Alberta, with warnings about high water levels in the Bow River and the potential for flooding in Banff National Park.
